title: No error while setting scrape_interval to an empty string (empty config silently breaks scrape jobs)
summary: Setting scrape_interval="" (empty string) via juju config returns exit 0 and shows no error in juju status, but the empty value is pushed verbatim into every scrape job. Prometheus then rejects the config ('empty duration string') and the affected scrape job silently disappears. Root cause: _prometheus_configurations builds the override dict from self.model.config.items() (src/charm.py:136) and merges it into each job via job.update(config) (src/charm.py:143) without filtering empty values.
why_good_first_issue: Well-scoped single-file bug with a clear repro in the issue and an obvious fix surface (one method, src/charm.py:125-155). No deep charm/architecture knowledge needed; existing unit tests already exercise this method, so a regression test slots straight in alongside test_change_scrape_interval.
affected_files: src/charm.py:136 (config dict comprehension), src/charm.py:143 (job.update(config)); config schema in charmcraft.yaml (scrape_interval/scrape_timeout/proxy_url etc. are type=string with no default)
repro_steps: On VM: cloned repo, added a temp test mirroring tests/unit/test_charm.py::test_change_scrape_interval but with harness.update_config({'scrape_interval': ''}), then asserted the rendered downstream scrape_jobs. Ran: PYTHONPATH=.:lib:src uv run --frozen --isolated --extra=dev python -m pytest tests/unit/test_repro_empty.py -v -s.
repro_result: REPRODUCED (unit). Output: 'RENDERED scrape_interval repr: ""' — i.e. jobs[0]['scrape_interval'] == '' (empty string present in the rendered job). Observed vs expected: observed = empty string propagated verbatim into the scrape job (key present, value ''); expected-after-fix = empty config value dropped so the key is absent (and/or a BlockedStatus surfaced). Baseline suite: 16 passed (tox -e unit). Matches the issue's prometheus error 'empty duration string'.
fix_hint: In _prometheus_configurations (src/charm.py:136) skip empty/whitespace-only string values when building config, e.g. config = {k: v for k, v in self.model.config.items() if k not in [...] and not (isinstance(v, str) and v.strip() == '')}. Optionally set BlockedStatus to surface an invalid empty value to the operator. Add a regression unit test asserting the key is absent when scrape_interval='' .
notes: ops resolved 2.21.x; charm builds on Ubuntu 24.04. Issue still open & valid (labels Type: Bug, Status: Triage); not obsoleted by current code (no empty-value filtering exists, confirmed via grep).

title: Use modern ops main entrypoint: replace from ops.main import main with from ops import main
summary: src/charm.py uses the deprecated entrypoint: from ops.main import main (line 24) and main(PrometheusScrapeConfigCharm) (line 173). ops>=2 recommends from ops import main.
why_good_first_issue: Two-line mechanical change in a single file; modern API is unambiguous and matches house style across COS charms; unit tests cover the charm so a regression would surface.
repro_steps: grep -n 'ops.main' and 'main(' in src/charm.py -> line 24 from ops.main import main; line 173 main(PrometheusScrapeConfigCharm).
repro_result: Observed: src/charm.py:24 'from ops.main import main' and src/charm.py:173 'main(PrometheusScrapeConfigCharm)'. Expected (ops>=2): 'from ops import main'. ops floor in pyproject.toml is >=2.x so the modern form is available; resolved ops 2.21.x.
fix_hint: Replace line 24 with 'from ops import main'; keep the call on line 173. Run tox -e unit and tox -e static.
notes: Secondary, lower-priority cleanup; same pattern recurs across these single-purpose charms. Baseline unit suite: 16 passed.
